query: I'm building an ECS and can't decide how to store components — should each entity own its components or should I group entities by which components they have?
rationale: direct: archetype/bitmask storage vs per-entity ownership

query: our physics actors lag a frame behind the game — a fast car gets missed by raycasts. how should I push transform changes into PhysX from my component data?
rationale: direct: mirroring ECS state to a stateful external system, out-of-sync window

query: can an entity have two of the same component, like two colliders, or should I model that some other way?
rationale: direct: single-vs-multiple components verdict and alternatives

query: I have a system that updates a few hundred thousand objects and I want it to run across all my worker threads without locking — how do I structure the loop?
rationale: implicit: filter-and-batch system loop + parallel jobs, no 'ECS' said

query: we register on-change callbacks so the renderer updates whenever a component is edited, but now our job scheduler hits races — what's the right pattern?
rationale: implicit: callbacks break parallelism; use brute-force/change-lists

query: how should rendering components hook into the engine so I can cull lots of meshes per camera and feed several shadow views in one pass?
rationale: implicit: ECS->renderer interfaces, per-viewer visibility bitmasks

query: restructure this CPU particle array from array-of-structs to struct-of-arrays so the update loop stops missing cache
rationale: near-miss: raw CPU data layout -> data-oriented-design-guide, not ECS architecture

query: design the render graph passes and barrier/transition derivation for my Vulkan deferred renderer
I'm modeling thousands of units in an RTS and want systems that iterate only the units that have both health and movement components — how do I match and walk them?
rationale: validation positive: component-mask filtering + co-located iteration

query: what's the cheapest way to know which entities changed this frame so my audio engine only re-syncs those, without slowing down the parallel systems?
rationale: validation positive: change-tracking strategies, dirty flags vs change lists vs tags

query: refactor my GameObject base class and its Update() override hierarchy so subclasses share behavior through inheritance
rationale: validation negative: class/inheritance game-object design -> general-oop-guide

query: tune this LuaJIT table layout so the hot path stays JIT-friendly and avoids rehashing
rationale: validation negative: LuaJIT table tuning -> lua-opinionated-guide
